Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Dismissal Pursuant To C.C.P 473(B)
Off Calendar Nov-20-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ANGELES BERMUDEZ'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFFS REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS (PART 2 OF 2, TENTATIVE RULING)
Off Calendar Mar-04-2024 Off Calendar

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Mar-04-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ANGELES BERMUDEZ'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And Request For Sanctions

Plaintiff filed the underlying Song-Beverly action on September 14, 2023, alleging breach of express and implied warranty on the basis of Defendant Ford Motor's alleged failure to repair or repurchase a 2022 Ford Explorer at issue.

On October 31, 2023, Plaintiff propounded a set of document requests seeking all documents relating to: (i) the vehicle and the communications between Plaintiff and Defendant (RFP Nos. 1-14); (ii) Defendant's warranty, repurchase policies, procedures and practices (RPF Nos. 15-29); and (iii) Defendant's knowledge of the same or similar defects in other vehicles of the same year, make, and model as the subject vehicle (RFP Nos. 30-31). Defendant provided verified responses to Plaintiff's RFPs on December 1, 2023, and allegedly refused to produce documents in response to RFP Nos. 16-31.

Plaintiff sent a meet-and-confer letter on or about December 5, 2023. In the letter, Plaintiff attached an Addendum to the Case Management Order for Song-Beverly Litigation by Judges Hofer and Lu of Los Angeles County Superior Court in support of her argument for production. The Addendum lists a category of documents that both judges ordered Defendant to voluntarily produce in a Song-Beverly litigation.

Defendant responded on December 7, 2023, stating that it would provide a substantive response by December 27, 2023. Plaintiff then filed this motion to compel on December 13, 2023. On December 22, 2023, Defendant offered to produce the documents outlined in the Addendum.

It is apparent that this discovery issue has been litigated and resolved in other courts, one particular example being the Addendum referenced in Plaintiff's December 5, 2023 meet-and-confer letter. In light of Defendant's agreement to produce the document in accordance with the Addendum, Plaintiff's motion is DENIED as moot.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem Ken Korea at ken@colev.law.

Judge Pro Tem: Ken Korea; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
